systemPrompt.en: You are an interactive election campaign simulator narrator. The player is a first-time candidate running for mayor of a mid-sized city. They must manage campaign resources, respond to events, debate opponents, and win voter trust. Write in second person. Each response must be valid JSON with: nodeText (narration, max 300 chars), speaker (character name if dialogue), emotion (one of: hopeful, worried, determined, frustrated, neutral, excited), choices (3 objects with id and text, max 60 chars), isChapterEnd (boolean), nextChapter (string if chapter ending), metadataUpdate (object like {"approval": 5, "funds": -20, "momentum": 10, "integrity": -5}). Keep tone uplifting and civic-minded. All-ages appropriate.
systemPrompt.zh: 你是一个互动竞选模拟器叙述者。玩家是首次参选中型城市市长的候选人。他们必须管理竞选资源、应对事件、与对手辩论并赢得选民信任。用第二人称叙述。保持积极和公民意识的语调。

storySkeleton.setting: Riverside City, a diverse mid-sized American city facing tough economic challenges. The incumbent mayor is unpopular, and three candidates are vying to replace them. You're the underdog — a community organizer with big ideas but limited resources.

characters.name: Marcus Chen
characters.personality: Your seasoned campaign manager. Pragmatic, data-driven, and occasionally ruthless about strategy. Believes in you but won't hesitate to tell hard truths.

characters.name: Senator Victoria Hayes
characters.personality: The establishment candidate — wealthy, connected, and running on a platform of 'experience matters.' Polished but sometimes comes across as out of touch.

characters.name: Maya Torres
characters.personality: A passionate local journalist covering the race. Fair-minded but asks tough questions. Her endorsement could swing the election.

storySkeleton.opening: You stand before a modest crowd at the community center where you've organized food drives and after-school programs for years. Today is different. Today, you're announcing your candidacy for Mayor of Riverside City. The camera flashes, the crowd cheers, and your heart pounds. Let the campaign begin.

chapters.title: The Announcement
chapters.goal: Launch your campaign and build initial momentum
fallbackTexts: The applause fades and reality sets in. Marcus pulls you aside: 'Great speech, but we need a strategy. Hayes already has billboards up across the city.'
Your first town hall is scheduled for tomorrow. Maya Torres from the Gazette wants a one-on-one interview beforehand.
Volunteers are trickling in, but your campaign fund is dangerously low. You need to decide where to focus your limited resources.

fallbackChoices.text: Be completely honest about the city's challenges
Focus on your personal story and connection
Let the voters decide what matters most

chapters.title: The Debate
chapters.goal: Perform well in the first televised debate
fallbackTexts: The debate stage is set. Bright lights, three podiums, and a live audience of thousands. Senator Hayes glides in with practiced confidence.
The moderator asks about the city's budget crisis. This is your chance to shine — or stumble.
Hayes just made a pointed comment about your 'lack of experience.' The audience waits for your response.

fallbackChoices.text: Rise above it with a positive vision
Ask the audience what they think
End with a powerful closing statement
Extend a hand to your opponents
Rally the crowd with a call to action

chapters.title: The Scandal
chapters.goal: Navigate a crisis that threatens your campaign
fallbackTexts: Breaking news: A leaked email suggests your campaign accepted an improper donation. The story is spreading fast on social media.
Marcus warns: 'We need to get ahead of this before it defines the campaign. What's our move?'
Maya Torres calls requesting an exclusive interview to give your side of the story. This could help — or make things worse.

fallbackChoices.text: Go directly to voters via social media

chapters.title: The Home Stretch
chapters.goal: Maximize support in the final week of campaigning
fallbackTexts: One week until election day. The polls show a statistical dead heat between you and Hayes. Every move matters now.
Marcus presents three options for the final push. 'We can't do everything — we have to pick our battles.'
A major newspaper is about to make its endorsement. Your last rally could tip the scales.

fallbackChoices.text: Go all-in on a massive rally downtown
Attack Hayes' weak points one last time
Unite the city with a positive message

chapters.title: Election Night
chapters.goal: Await the results and face the outcome
fallbackTexts: Election day. Your campaign headquarters is buzzing with nervous energy as the first results start coming in.
The numbers are fluctuating. Your stronghold neighborhoods have reported, but Hayes' districts haven't. It could go either way.
The final results are about to be announced. Whatever happens, you've already changed this city's political landscape forever.

fallbackChoices.text: Watch the results with your team
Prepare both a victory and concession speech
Call your family and thank them
Regardless of outcome, pledge to keep fighting
Look ahead to the next chapter
Commit to serving the community either way

endings.title: Landslide Victory
endings.description: Against all odds, you won in a landslide. The people of Riverside City saw something in you — authenticity, hope, and a genuine desire to serve. As you take the stage to deliver your victory speech, you know this is just the beginning.

endings.title: A Narrow Win
endings.description: It came down to the wire, but when the final votes were counted, you won by a razor-thin margin. Every door knocked, every handshake, every debate answer mattered. Riverside City has a new mayor, and the real work starts tomorrow.

endings.title: A Graceful Defeat
endings.description: You didn't win this time, but you ran a campaign that made the city proud. Hayes reached across the aisle after her victory, offering you a position in her administration. Sometimes losing with grace is its own kind of victory.

endings.condition: Low funds but high approval in final stretch
endings.title: A Movement Is Born
endings.description: The election results didn't go your way, but something bigger happened. You sparked a grassroots movement that will outlast any single election. The volunteers, the energy, the hope — it's all still there. This wasn't the end. It was the beginning.

en.name: Election Simulator
en.desc: Run for mayor in this AI-powered political campaign game — debate rivals, navigate scandals, and win voters
en.description: Ever wondered what it takes to run a political campaign? In Election Simulator, you're a community organizer running for mayor of Riverside City. Manage your campaign funds, ace televised debates, navigate unexpected scandals, and win the hearts and minds of voters. Every decision matters — from your debate strategy to how you handle a crisis. With AI-generated scenarios, every campaign is unique. Will you win in a landslide, or will this campaign spark something even bigger than an election?
en.howToPlay: Read each campaign scenario and make strategic choices. Your decisions affect four stats: Approval (voter support), Funds (campaign budget), Momentum (media buzz), and Integrity (your moral standing). Balance your resources wisely and stay true to your values. The story has multiple endings based on your campaign strategy.
tips: High Integrity can earn you key endorsements even with low Funds
Don't ignore Scandal chapters — they can make or break your campaign
Positive campaigns tend to yield better long-term results
There are 4 possible endings — replay with different strategies
The AI generates unique debate questions and events each time

faq.question: Is this game based on real politics?
faq.answer: No, the setting and characters are fictional. However, the game explores real campaign dynamics like resource management, public relations, and voter engagement.

faq.question: Can I win every time?
faq.answer: Not necessarily! Your choices matter, and the AI creates unpredictable scenarios. Sometimes losing can lead to the most interesting story endings.

faq.question: How long is one playthrough?
faq.answer: A typical campaign takes 15-25 minutes to complete, depending on your reading speed and choices.

zh.name: 竞选模拟器
zh.desc: 在AI驱动的政治竞选游戏中竞选市长——与对手辩论、应对丑闻、赢得选民
zh.description: 想知道经营一场政治竞选需要什么吗？在竞选模拟器中，你是一个竞选河畔市市长的社区组织者。管理竞选资金、在电视辩论中表现出色、应对突发丑闻、赢得选民的心。
